column1: The first Doom shall be Fire,     which shall burn the very stone, The next is Air, and rocks are overthrown, Earth shall be the third,     then the final Doom is heard, The Water gone, the city parched like bone.
column2: The first Doom shall be Fire,     which shall burn the very stone, The next is Air, and rocks are overthrown, Earth shall be the third,     then the final Doom is heard, The Water gone, the city parched like bone.

column1: Unless the one called Hero is a Hero true indeed, Who comes to help the city in its need, Then will face the depths of Doom     in the darkness of the Tomb, From the Elemental's Master, we are freed.
column2: Unless the one called Hero is a Hero true indeed, Who comes to help the city in its need, Then will face the depths of Doom     in the darkness of the Tomb, From the Elemental's Master, we are freed.

column1: Came a Hero drawing near     to our land of fair Shapeir, The Katta called and carried that one here. I shall tell an allegory     'bout a Quest for Love and Glory Which I shall relate if you will lend an ear.
column2: Came a Hero drawing near     to our land of fair Shapeir, The Katta called and carried that one here. I shall tell an allegory     'bout a Quest for Love and Glory Which I shall relate if you will lend an ear.

column1: The Hero first faced fire     which was making matters dire, For the flames he fought were flitting ever higher. With water first he fought it     and into a lamp he caught it 'Ere our city of Shapeir became our pyre.
column2: The Hero first faced fire     which was making matters dire, For the flames he fought were flitting ever higher. With water first he fought it     and into a lamp he caught it 'Ere our city of Shapeir became our pyre.

column1: The next he faced was Air,     which I'm sure that you're aware, Was blustering and blowing here and there. Before the Winds became our bane     as a fearsome hurricane, With a bellows then he caught it fair and square.
column2: The next he faced was Air,     which I'm sure that you're aware, Was blustering and blowing here and there. Before the Winds became our bane     as a fearsome hurricane, With a bellows then he caught it fair and square.

column1: So let us raise a cheer for the Hero of Shapeir, And remember that our praise is quite sincere, For in the Quest for Glory as related by this story, Is a Hero who has saved us from our fear, Is a Hero who's a Hero without peer!
column2: So let us raise a cheer for the Hero of Shapeir, And remember that our praise is quite sincere, For in the Quest for Glory as related by this story, Is a Hero who has saved us from our fear, Is a Hero who's a Hero without peer!

column1: In the Month of the Serpent,     in the Year of the Spear, Darkness passed over the town of Raseir, And the rumor had been said     that the old Emir was dead, For Arus al-Din had seemed to disappear.
column2: In the Month of the Serpent,     in the Year of the Spear, Darkness passed over the town of Raseir, And the rumor had been said     that the old Emir was dead, For Arus al-Din had seemed to disappear.

column1: The Emir had a brother,     who quickly took the throne, but the Shadow lurking under soon was known For the Katta were exiled,     and Raseir was then defiled, By the rules and regulations that were shown.
column2: The Emir had a brother,     who quickly took the throne, but the Shadow lurking under soon was known For the Katta were exiled,     and Raseir was then defiled, By the rules and regulations that were shown.

column1: The Sultan sent his men to restore Raseir to right, But they did not even have a chance to fight, For as they crossed the land,     a tremendous storm of sand, Forced his forces to retreat from their plight.
column2: The Sultan sent his men to restore Raseir to right, But they did not even have a chance to fight, For as they crossed the land,     a tremendous storm of sand, Forced his forces to retreat from their plight.

column1: So now the rumor's told of the city of Raseir, That the Darkness shadows everything in fear. And He that now does rule has the Emir as his tool, Disobedience brings punishment severe.
column2: So now the rumor's told of the city of Raseir, That the Darkness shadows everything in fear. And He that now does rule has the Emir as his tool, Disobedience brings punishment severe.

column1: The moral of this story as the best of tales must tell Of the horrors of the orders that befell, Is the simple corollary that the Hero must be wary, 'Ere He journeys to that Shadow's Citadel, And we bid him luck and wish him to fare well.
column2: The moral of this story as the best of tales must tell Of the horrors of the orders that befell, Is the simple corollary that the Hero must be wary, 'Ere He journeys to that Shadow's Citadel, And we bid him luck and wish him to fare well.

You will find things of interest there, but know that there are those who sleep in the next room.  Be wary, and if you are very good, we will both share your profits.

column1: Raseir was once a place where anyone with some brains and skills could make some quick cash.  Now that things have changed under the new Emir, only those who toady to the authorities can get ahead.  Ferrari is an excellent example of that type of person -- only too willing to kiss and tell.

column1: You have the bellows.  What more do you want?
column2: You have the bellows.  What more do you want?

column1: You must need my bellows badly.
column2: You must need my bellows badly.

column1: Well, since you put it that way, I guess you do need the bellows.  I'll get it for you.
column2: Well, since you put it that way, I guess you do need the bellows.  I'll get it for you.

column1: Take it or leave it.
column2: Take it or leave it.

column1: You told me the story already, and besides, you have the bellows.  What else do you want?
column2: You told me the story already, and besides, you have the bellows.  What else do you want?

column1: Do tell.  Well, if you want to use my bellows, then you've got to prove your worth.  You'll have to defeat me at Arm Wrestling.  You win, you get the bellows.  You lose, I get 20 dinars.
column2: Do tell.  Well, if you want to use my bellows, then you've got to prove your worth.  You'll have to defeat me at Arm Wrestling.  You win, you get the bellows.  You lose, I get 20 dinars.

column1: There's no one to wrestle with.  Wasn't the purpose of breaking in to steal something?
column2: There's no one to wrestle with.  Wasn't the purpose of breaking in to steal something?

column1: It will cost you another 20 dinar to wrestle me for the bellows.
column2: It will cost you another 20 dinar to wrestle me for the bellows.

column1: It'll cost you more to bet me this time.  You'll have to put down %d Dinars.
